arts.ttl: Report: Bayern interested in Premier League goalkeeper
arts.sum: Bart Verbruggen has been linked with a move to the Allianz Arena if reports from England are to be believed.

arts.ttl: Report: Things not looking good for Bayern Munich’s Manuel Neuer after injury setback
arts.sum: The re-aggravated calf issue for Bayern Munich goalkeeper Manuel Neuer might be worse than originally thought per a report from Bild (via @iMiaSanMia ): Looking for more thoughts and discussions on Bayern Munich’s plan to work around the injuries to Alphonso Davies, Dayot Upamecano, and Manuel Neuer?

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich vs. St. Pauli : Lineups, team news, injuries, and more!
arts.sum: It’s basically the best possible lineup Bayern can put out against Pauli. The bombshell news that Bayern just got is that Alphonso Davies and Dayot Upamecano are both out for extended periods of time (Davies up to 9 months, Upamecano up to 6 weeks), which is unwelcome news for Vincent Kompany.

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Newcastle ready to push for in-demand £40m striker likened to Harry Kane as Callum Wilson replacement
arts.sum: TBR Football can exclusively confirm Liam Delap’s transfer stance amid interest from the likes of Newcastle United, Chelsea, Arsenal, Manchester United and other Premier League...

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich consider legal action against Canada Soccer over Davies injury
arts.sum: Davies tore ACL during Nations League third-place game Club executives demand ‘full investigation into events’ Bayern Munich are considering legal action against the Canadian soccer federation after Alphonso Davies sustained a serious knee injury in the Concacaf Nations League third-place playoff on Sunday. “We’re demanding a full investigation into the events from Canada Soccer and expressly reserve the right to take legal action,” the Bayern chief executive, Jan-Christian Dreesen, told the German newspaper Bild on Friday. Davies tore his anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) and sustained other damage to his right knee during Canada’s 2-1 win over the United States. The full-back returned to Munich where the full extent of the injury was determined and “will be out for several months”, according to his club. Bayern Munich are alleging that Canadian officials did not provide appropriate care for the player by starting him in “a match of no sporting significance”. Both teams had lost in the tournament semi-finals, Canada beaten by eventual winners Mexico and the US losing to Panama. “Sending a clearly injured player with a damaged knee on a 12-hour flight without a thorough medical assessment is, in our view, grossly negligent and a clear breach of medical duty of care,” Dreesen said. “The participation of Davies, who already had muscular problems before the game, in a match of no sporting significance is incomprehensible from our point of view.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: FIFA forced to compensate Bayern Munich as club fume over 'negligence'
arts.sum: Bayern Munich have confirmed the news that Alphonso Davies will miss the rest of the season with a serious knee injury having picked up an issue while on international duty with Canada

arts.ttl: Weekend Warm-up: Is Bayern Munich’s Vincent Kompany to blame for slew of injury setbacks?; Throwing it back to The Jim Carroll Band; Bundesliga predictions; Bayern eyeing PSG’s Bradley Barcola?; and MORE!
arts.sum: If — by chance — you have been listening to Bavarian Podcast Works this season, you have become familiar with one host (me...it’s me) consistently asking why there have been so many injury setbacks and calling Bayern Munich on the carpet for its handling of injured players. Kim Min-jae has been dealing with Achilles tendonitis since at least November and had a chance to rest for more than two weeks if Bayern Munich handled him properly during this international break.
